meta.note: 参照 AIGC-ZEN-VOID 场景面板模板迁入当前仓，用于 ScenePanel 提示词合成。

constraints: Render one coherent scene as a unified 3x3 panel sheet with strict cross-panel consistency.
No split style: all panels must share the same architectural era, material logic, lighting source direction, and color script.
Treat every panel as a view variant of the same space, not nine different scenes.

layout_generation_prompt.canvas_setup: Create a 16:9 widescreen scene sheet (4096x2304) on a dark neutral ground using a strict 3x3 nine-grid. Keep spacing, margins, and panel ratio uniform, but let the sheet behave like a cultural-spatial dossier rather than a purely technical orthographic board.
critical_requirements: The upstream scene design prompt is the primary source of truth; layout instructions organize the sheet but must not override scene identity, era, style, spatial facts, or narrative function.
Reference images, when provided, are continuity anchors only; do not copy unrelated composition, accidental artifacts, or subject drift from references.
P1-P3: atmosphere plus iconography sequence (wide, medium, ritual detail).

P4/P5/P7/P8: structural or directional views, but at least two panels must foreground era-coded cultural thresholds, ornament, ritual zoning, edge traversal, or cosmology linkage rather than empty technical coverage.
P6: low-angle up-shot emphasizing vertical authority, oppression, or transcendence; P9: high-angle top-down shot revealing spatial order or cosmology logic.
Maintain anchor architecture and landmark identity across all nine panels; silhouette, position logic, material, and symbolic anchors cannot drift.
Maintain one consistent spatial orientation across all panels; doors, thresholds, major landmarks, and light direction must not flip randomly between views.
Include at least one scale-readable environmental cue such as steps, railing height, doorway size, furniture scale, floor tile rhythm, or structural measurement logic, without adding people.
Keep empty-scene guardrail: no humans, creatures, silhouettes, or human-shaped shadows.
When the scene belongs to a mythic, religious, or historical world, at least three panels must visibly encode culture-specific symbols, materials, or ceremonial circulation logic.
If the design direction implies sacred luminosity, preserve brightness, sanctity, and material cleanliness; do not inject ruin, mildew, bunker heaviness, or generic dark-fantasy decay.

If the design direction implies a liminal passage or world-linking corridor, show openness, edge traversal, and cosmology connection; do not collapse the space into a fortress gatehouse, prison tower, or sealed bunker corridor.
Panel labels, grid divisions, and borders must remain clean and secondary; do not let labels, decorative UI, or frame ornaments dominate the scene.

Must reserve one fixed top-left identity badge area using matched roster text `<SCENE_ID>+<SCENE_NAME>`; if exact text rendering is unreliable, preserve the badge area and short ID cleanly for post-production text overlay.

modules.label: Atmosphere Wide
modules.description: Eye-level wide shot establishing full spatial envelope and dominant light logic.

modules.label: Atmosphere Medium
modules.description: Mid shot showing functional zoning, narrative center, and the first clear era/culture cue.

modules.label: Ritual Detail
modules.description: Close architectural or material detail that proves iconography, ceremonial logic, or world-specific ornament.

modules.label: Directional / Threshold Views
modules.description: Directional consistency views that prove threshold design, circulation hierarchy, cosmology order, edge traversal, or sacred-light organization without defaulting to fortress architecture.

modules.label: Vertical Axis Views
modules.description: One low-angle upward frame and one high-angle top-down frame for spatial verification and mythic or social power reading.

modules.label: Lighting Continuity
modules.description: Primary light source direction and intensity must remain physically coherent in all panels.

modules.label: Material Continuity
modules.description: Material families and texture age state must remain stable across panels.

modules.label: Layout Discipline
modules.description: Keep equal panel sizes, clean gutters, no overlap, no decorative frame noise.
